sections.paragraph: not used in Kal. Piel, to be exhausted, void of strength, 1Sa 30:10, 21 1Sa 30:21 (Talmud to be lazy, at ease; Syr. ܒܓܰܪ (the letters ב and פ being interchanged), to be attenuated; ܒܓܝܺܪܳܐ weak, wearied. Also the Lat. piger. The primary stock of both words פגר and piger, is פג; compare פּוּג, piget). Hence-
